steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: The key insight is that a Foucault pendulum appears to change its swing path over time due to Earth's rotation, not because of changes in its physical setup. This point directly connects the mechanics of the pendulum with evidence for planetary movement.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice B — Though it swings on an unchanging path, a Foucault pendulum appears to change its swing path over time because Earth rotates beneath it. captures this exact idea: "Though it swings on an unchanging path, a Foucault pendulum appears to change its swing path over time because Earth rotates beneath it." This explicitly links the observed change to Earth's rotation, providing the mechanistic explanation the question requires. ✓

  • Choice A — In Krakow, Poland, a Foucault pendulum at Jagiellonian University provides evidence of Earth's rotation.: "In Krakow, Poland, a Foucault pendulum at Jagiellonian University provides evidence of Earth's rotation." This asserts that the pendulum provides evidence but explains nothing about HOW it does so — it omits Earth's rotation as the cause of the apparent swing-path change. ✗
  • Choice C — A Foucault pendulum, which consists of a weighted ball at the end of a long cable, provides evidence of Earth's rotation.: "A Foucault pendulum, which consists of a weighted ball at the end of a long cable, provides evidence of Earth's rotation." This focuses on the physical description (cable, weighted ball) without explaining the mechanism by which Earth's rotation causes the apparent swing-path shift. ✗
  • Choice D — The Foucault pendulum at Jagiellonian University in Krakow, Poland, swings on an unchanging path, but Earth rotates beneath it.: "The Foucault pendulum at Jagiellonian University in Krakow, Poland, swings on an unchanging path, but Earth rotates beneath it." This mentions both facts but drops the critical link — it never states that Earth's rotation causes observers to see a changing swing path, so it does not explain HOW the pendulum provides evidence. ✗
That matches Choice B — Though it swings on an unchanging path, a Foucault pendulum appears to change its swing path over time because Earth rotates beneath it..
